long_description:
Issue running VSI/BM regression in dal09. 
Jira - FABREQ-75827
short_description: Issue running VSI/BM regression in dal09
close_notes: The issue was found to be with a specific agent node running on devbuilddal1201, for which the configs were pointed to the DC WDC04. The config was changed back to point to DAL10 resulting in resolution
current_status_next_steps: This is being investigated with the help of DevAuto
chronology:
Timelines are in UTC

03.12.2025

07:00 UTC - Outage Start / Initial Report: Compute QA reported in #sl-fabric-sre that they are unable to execute provisions for Bare Metal (BM) Regression in staging.
06:30 UTC - Core team inquired about activity on stagingdal0901, initiating a broader discussion.
13:30 UTC - Compute QA informed about the issue - "Memcached add operation failed with error #47: 'SERVER HAS FAILED AND IS DISABLED UNTIL TIMED RETRY'"
14:22 UTC - It was identified the code in question was written by CORE, not Engine. Cheryll confirmed that if this testing fails, the release cannot proceed. Decision made to raise an Incident (INC).
14:30 UTC - Incident ticket created and shared with the team.
15:01 UTC - Incident raised to Sev3 Severity
15:00 UTC - The teams call for the warroom was started and the notification was sent out in the warroom chat.
15:02 UTC - Jeff and Allen began investigation. Discovered that both the Release and Stable branches were pointing at WDC04 (an inactive Data Center).
15:06 UTC - DevAuto team called in for assistance.
15:15 UTC - It was found the specific issue is with the san-test agent node running on devbuilddal1201.softlayer.local. Environment configs were pointing to WDC04
15:20 UTC - Configuration chages made to point to DAL10 for testing purposes.
15:25 UTC - Following the fix, the Staging build ran successfully. Cheryll ran the Release build. FSRE requested the Compute QA team to test VM/BM Regression and VSI tests immediately.
15:26 UTC - Health Check: Engine team started Engine Health Checks.
15:31 UTC - Compute QA started VSI and BM regression tests. Jeff and Allen requested DBA Oncall support. Needed to verify database integrity because production data and recovery data from DAL10 were both being written to it.
15:32 UTC - Allen removed labels from the nodes to ensure they do not pick up tasks even if they come online.
15:36 UTC - DBA Oncall joined the bridge. DBA confirmed they can run a program to generate a report identifying faulty transactions from the last 20 hours (estimated runtime: 2-3 days).
15:38 UTC - Compute QA confirmed all tests are running correctly. One failure detected, but confirmed as an unrelated BM issue. DBA started the script (expected to run for the next 2 days).
15:47 UTC - Apache bounced on Staging.

4th Dec 2025
- 2025-12-04 07:06 UTC (12:36 PM IST)- Compute team reported the issue that the release branch got deleted somehow and only the stable branch was found. The issue was escalated to DevAuto.
- 2025-12-04 06:56 UTC (12:53 PM IST)- FSRE created and escalated the jira for tracking to DevAuto. FABREQ-75912 User not seeing any jira branch in staging0901
- 2025-12-04 07:32 UTC (01:02PM IST)- DevAuto found that the Weekly_Delete_Staging_Branches has run which has deleted the Release branch from the staging server and after that the Deploy Stable Staging job has run as a downstream job thus only the stable branch is there and not the release branch. 
- 2025-12-04 07:34 UTC (01:04PM IST)- Compute asked to deploy the latest release branch as they were doing BM regression. FSRE asked them to update the same in the warroom. 
- 2025-12-04 07:50 UTC (01:20PM IST)- FSRE enquired from compute that VSI regression has completed testing so will it need to start again. Compute informed that only a sanity check would be enough, only BM testing needs to be performed. 
- 2025-12-04 08:14 UTC (01:44PM IST)- The teams call was initiated and the stakeholders to join it.
- 2025-12-04 12:10 UTC (05:39 PM IST)- Cheryll enquired Compute team about the time that it will take for BM regression to complete testing. Compute gave an approximation of 8-10 hours. 
- 2025-12-04 12:15 UTC (05:54 PM IST)- Cheryll completed rebuilding the release build from scratch and Compute triggered BM provisioning.
